mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when either series shows a concurrent decline, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over a five-day horizon.
failure_condition: The positive signal reverses to negative returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, demonstrating regime dependence that invalidates the unconditional positive claim.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 238.0 indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 70% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 238.0 threshold, negating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when the unemployment rate falls while payrolls stagnate, or when the interaction effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent reflects sustained macroeconomic expansion that underpins corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns show zero or negative correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates across a rolling 10-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that triggers recession fears and forces immediate negative repricing of S&P 500 equities over five trading days.
failure_condition: The market fails to decline over the subsequent five days in more than 60% of instances where the industrial production threshold is breached, indicating the signal is noise rather than a leading indicator.
